question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘How Family Shapes One’s Future’ in 10 sentences. 10
solution_html:
How Family Shapes One’s Future

Family plays a vital role in shaping a person’s character and future, as it is the first place where we learn values and discipline. Parents guide us in making wise decisions and help us develop confidence through their constant support. A loving family environment encourages dreams, ambition, and the courage to face life’s challenges. Children raised with care and affection usually grow up with strong morals and emotional stability. On the other hand, a broken family can harm a child’s growth and weaken its sense of responsibility. Families teach honesty, empathy, respect, and other qualities essential for success in life. Supportive family members inspire us to learn from our failures instead of giving up easily. Every word and action in a family leaves a lasting impact on a child’s personality. The encouragement we receive at home often turns small efforts into meaningful achievements. Truly, our future is largely shaped by the love, guidance, and lessons we receive from our family.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Mark --------------------09 Marks

(A) For Title (How Family Shapes One’s Future) -------- 01 Mark

(B) Topic Sentence (Introduction) ---------------------------01 Mark

Family plays a vital role in shaping a person’s character and future, as it is the first place where we learn values and discipline.
‎C) Context Explanation (How Family Shapes One’s Future) ( 06 points)------06 Marks
‎• It serves as the primary source for learning values and discipline.
‎• Parents provide guidance in decision-making and instill confidence through support.
‎• A nurturing family environment fosters dreams, ambition, and resilience in facing challenges.
‎• Children from loving families tend to develop strong morals and emotional stability.
‎• Conversely, a broken family can negatively affect growth and responsibility.
‎• Families impart essential qualities such as honesty, empathy, and respect, vital for success.
‎• Supportive family members encourage perseverance and resilience in the face of failure.
‎• Every interaction within a family leaves a lasting impression on a child's personality.
‎• Home encouragement transforms small efforts into significant achievements.

D) Concluding Sentence ------------01 Mark
‎Truly, our future is largely shaped by the love, guidance, and lessons we receive from our family.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 12 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 12 sentences but less than double (20 sentences), 50% of the deserving marks must be deducted. If it becomes double (20 sentences), full marks have to be deducted. Comment: Write within 10 sentences.

3. Students must write within a para. If a student writes more than one para, half of the deserving marks must be deducted. (Comment: Write within single/one para).

4. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark. (comment: Write the Title/ How Family Shapes One’s Future)

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with suitable words: 5

Conflict is an (a)_______ part of life. It is as varied as (b)________ that contribute to it. All of us have our (c)________ opinions, ideas and beliefs. We also have our own way of looking at things and we act according to what we think is (d)________. If, however, we are too (e)_______ about these, and do not allow others to freely express their opinions and beliefs, or even the right to hold them, conflict becomes inevitable.
solution_html:
(a) inevitable

(b) causes

(c) own

(d) proper

(e) rigid

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with the clues given in the box. There are more words than necessary. Make any grammatical changes (if necessary). 5

A warrior of light (a) _____ his world with the people he loves. He tries to (b) _____ them to do the things they (c) _____ like to but for which they lack the courage; at such time, the enemy (d) _____ holding two wooden signs in his hand. On one sign is written: ‘Think about (e) _____. Keep all the blessings for yourself, otherwise you’ll end up losing everything’. On the other sign, he reads: ‘Who (f)_____ you (g) _____ you are, helping other people? Can’t you see your own faults?’ A warrior knows that he has faults. But he knows too that he cannot do his (h) _____ alone and thus distance himself from his companions. Therefore, he (i) _____ the signs to the floor, even if he thinks they may contain a grain of truth. The signs crumble into dust, and the warrior (j) _____ to encourage those nearest him.
solution_html:
(a) shares

(b) encourage

(c) would

(d) appears

(e) yourself

(f) do

(g) think

(h) growing

(i) throws

(j) continues

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Write meaningful sentences of your own with each of the following words: 10

Available, Disposition, Inaugurate, Prevent, Culmination, Vanity, Independently, Charisma, Prejudice, Appreciate
solution_html:
Available (Adjective): The information became easily available after the organization uploaded all documents to their website.

Disposition (Noun): Her cheerful disposition helped everyone stay motivated during the challenging group project.

Inaugurate (Verb): The city plans to inaugurate a new cultural center next month with a grand ceremony.

Prevent (Verb): Proper safety measures can prevent many accidents from occurring in busy workplaces.

Culmination (Noun): The event marked the culmination of months of hard work and careful planning.

Vanity (Noun): His excessive vanity often distracted him from focusing on more meaningful accomplishments.

Independently (Adverb): She completed the difficult assignment independently, demonstrating confidence and strong problem-solving skills.

Charisma (Noun): The leader’s natural charisma attracted supporters from various backgrounds and age groups.

Prejudice (Noun): Education plays a key role in reducing social prejudice and promoting mutual respect.

Prejudice (Verb): The prosecution lawyers have been trying to prejudice the jury against her. [Students can use it both as Noun and Verb]

Appreciate (Verb): We truly appreciate your dedication and the consistent effort you contribute every day.

Instructions:

(i) Any correct and logically meaningful sentence will be accepted. (Sentences with weak meaning will not be allowed.)

(ii) For any grammatical or structural mistake, full mark for this item must be deducted. Examiners must underline and correct the mistake(s) in the scripts.

(iii) For 02 spelling mistakes, 01 mark will be deducted, for 01 spelling mistake, 0.5 marks will be deducted from that very sentence.
‎(iv) For changing the part of speech of the given word by adding a suffix or prefix, the full marks have to be deducted from the very number. But students can use any form if the given word is a verb (depending on the subject-verb agreement, tense, person, number, etc.).

question_html:
Translate the following paragraph into English: 5

শিক্ষার লক্ষ্য হলো আমাদের আচরণে ইতিবাচক পরিবর্তন আনা। এটি আমাদের জীবন এবং আমরা যে সমাজে বসবাস করি তা পরিবর্তন করার চেষ্টা করতে সহায়তা করে। যে শিক্ষা আমাদের মনকে আলোকিত করে না অথবা আমাদের সমাজের জন্য কাজ করতে উদ্বুদ্ধ করে না তা কখনোই পূর্ণাঙ্গ শিক্ষা না। শিক্ষা মানে শুধু গ্রেড এবং সার্টিফিকেট অর্জনই নয়, আমরা জীবনকে উন্নত করতে শিক্ষা ব্যবহার করি। আমাদের কাছে প্রত্যাশা করা হয় যে, শ্রেণিকক্ষে শেখা জ্ঞান, দক্ষতা ও মূল্যবোধগুলোকে আমরা বাইরের জগতের সাথে সম্পৃক্ততার ক্ষেত্রে প্রয়োগ করবো। এবং এটি আমরা বিভিন্ন উপায়ে করতে পারি।
solution_html:
অনুবাদ: Education aims to bring about positive changes in our behavior. It helps us try to change our lives as well as the society we live in. Education that does not illuminate our minds or does not inspire us to work for the community is not complete at all. Education is not all about getting grades or receiving certificates- we use education to make life better. We are expected to apply the knowledge, skills and values that we learn in a classroom in our engagement with the world that lies outside. And we can do so in many different ways.

Instructions:
‎* For accurate translation using grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, students will get full marks. [05 marks for translating 05 sentences correctly]

* For every 03 grammatical/spelling errors 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html:
Add at least five more sentences to complete the following story. 5

Selim was walking to the bus stop when he noticed an old mango tree by the roadside. Its branches were heavy with unpicked fruit. He paused for a moment, remembering how he used to climb a similar tree in his village as a child. A little boy stood under the tree, staring up longingly at the ripe mangoes far beyond his reach. Seeing the boy’s quiet frustration reminded Selim of his own childhood desires, simple yet deeply felt......................
solution_html:
The Roadside Mango Tree

Selim was walking to the bus stop when he noticed an old mango tree by the roadside. Its branches were heavy with unpicked fruit. He paused for a moment, remembering how he used to climb a similar tree in his village as a child. A little boy stood under the tree, staring up longingly at the ripe mangoes far beyond his reach. Seeing the boy’s quiet frustration reminded Selim of his own childhood desires, simple yet deeply felt. Without thinking too much, he decided to help and offered to lift the boy onto his shoulders. The boy carefully picked two mangoes and handed one to Selim with a shy smile. They sat under the shade, eating the sun-warmed fruit in comfortable silence. For Selim, the moment felt like a brief return to a gentler time he thought he had lost. As he walked away, he felt lighter, as though he had shared more than just a mango. He realized that small, ordinary gestures often grow into memories that stay with us quietly, like fruit ripening in the sun.

Marks Distribution

Highest mark---------------05 Marks

(i) Title (Any logical one) ----(01 Mark has to be deducted for not writing the title) “The Roadside Mango Tree)

(ii) Story (minimum new 5 sentences) 1×5=05 Marks

{If a student adds or writes at least 5-7 new sentences using the given part and completes the story with a proper description without any grammatical or spelling mistakes, he/she will get full marks}.

Instructions:

(i) If a student doesn’t write the given part, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the given part first).

(ii) Students can write the story in any logical way (sample answer isn’t fixed).

(iii) The given part tells us that the story is the incidents of the Past. So, it is normal that the student will develop the story using Past Tense but it can be described in Present Tense and future Tense also by using direct speech.

(iv) If a student doesn’t write the Title, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the Title/ The Roadside Mango Tree)
